Agent Graph — Layout Options for Dense Graphs

Current: nodesep:40 + NODE_WIDTH:140 → 6 agents = 1040px wide. 12 agents = 2120px. Always overflows the panel.

Current — single-rank fan-out

Small panel (400px) — zoomed to fit
⚡ Agent Graph 8 ≡ Agent Log 1314

MAIN SESSION
running
● engineer
TASK-004: BIDIR…
running

TASK-008: FIX CMD…
✓ completed
● engineer
TASK-005: FIX AGENT…
✓ completed
● engineer
TASK-003: FIX RT…
✓ completed
● engineer
TASK-002: FIX RT…
✓ completed
● orchestrator
ORCHESTRATE FEED…
✓ completed
Agents: 8 Running: 2 Cost: $12.06
Nodes unreadable at fit-to-view. User sees blobs, not info.
Large empty canvas — 80% of the panel is empty space below nodes.
Width scales linearly — 12 agents → 2120px minimum.

Three layout strategies

Option 1 — Wrapped grid (max N cols)
⚡ Agent Graph 8 ≡ Agent Log
cols 3
● main
MAIN SESSION
running
● engineer
TASK-004: bidir…
running
● engineer
TASK-007: cmd dis…
running
● engineer
TASK-005: agents…
✓ done
● engineer
TASK-003: rt ne…
✓ done
● engineer
TASK-002: rt ag…
✓ done
● orchestrator
FEEDBACK-V2…
✓ done
Agents: 7 Running: 2 Cost: $12.06
Width capped at panel width. Rows wrap like CSS grid.
DAG structure preserved — edges still show parent→child.
Column count auto from: floor(panelWidth / (nodeWidth + gap)).
Implementation: replace dagre with custom grid layout when all nodes are depth ≤ 2.
Option 2 — Compact vertical list (flat trees)
⚡ Agent Graph 8
LIST VIEW
main running $12.06 · 117k tok
eng TASK-004: bidirectional-t… running
eng TASK-007: command-dispa… running
eng TASK-008: fix-cmd-dis… ✓ done
eng TASK-005: fix-agent-list… ✓ done
eng TASK-003: fix-realtime-ne… ✓ done
eng TASK-002: fix-realtime-ag… ✓ done
orch ORCHESTRATE-FEEDBACK-V2… ✓ done
rev REVIEW-TASK-004… ✓ done
Agents: 8 Running: 2 Cost: $12.06
Zero width overflow — works at any panel width.
Scrollable — 100 agents is just 100 rows.
Full task name visible on hover/expand. No zoom needed.
Trigger: auto-switch when panel width < 360px OR depth ≤ 1 (all agents are direct children of main).
Option 3 — Compact cards + constrained width
⚡ Agent Graph 8
compact full
main MAIN SESSION
eng TASK-004
eng TASK-007
eng TASK-008
eng TASK-005
eng TASK-003
orch FEEDBACK
ENGINEER TASK-007: fix command dispatch ● running
Agents: 7 Running: 2 Cost: $12.06
NODE_WIDTH shrinks to 100px in compact mode → 3 nodes fit in 320px.
Selected node details shown in a pinned strip below graph (no tooltip needed).
"Full" toggle restores big nodes for deep inspection.
Toggle stored in component state. Compact = default when nodeCount > 6.

Recommendation — combine 2 + 3

Tier 1 (depth ≤ 1, panel < 380px): Auto-switch to the compact vertical list (Option 2). Zero layout math, zero overflow, infinitely scalable. This covers the most common case: main → N parallel agents.

Tier 2 (depth ≥ 2, panel any width): Use compact node cards (Option 3) with cols = floor((panelWidth - 20) / (nodeWidth + gap)) rows to keep the graph within bounds. "Full" toggle for detail.

Tier 3 (deep nested graphs): Keep current dagre for complex multi-level trees (rare) but apply the compact node size by default.

Key code change: const cols = Math.max(1, Math.floor((panelPx - 20) / (NODE_W + NODE_GAP)))
Use a ResizeObserver on the panel container → feed width into layout function reactively.
List view: pure flexbox/div, no XYFlow/dagre overhead. Fast even for 50+ agents.
